title: Kelly Greenhill on Office Hours Podcast
summary: Kelly Greenhill, an Associate Professor at Tufts University and a Research Fellow with the Belfer Center’s International Security Project, talks with Aroop Mukharji (@aroopmukharji) about refugees, building walls, R2P, and weapons of mass migration.
body: Kelly M. Greenhill is a research fellow in the Belfer Center's International Security Program and an Associate Professor (with tenure) at Tufts University. Her research focuses on foreign policy, the use of military force, and what are frequently called "new security challenges," including civil wars; the use of forced migration as a weapon; military intervention and (counter-) insurgency; and international crime as a challenge to domestic governance. She holds a Ph.D. and an S.M. from M.I.T., a C.S.S. from Harvard University, and a B.A. (with distinction and highest honors) in Political Economy and in Scandinavian Studies from the University of California at Berkeley. Greenhill also serves as Chair of the Conflict, Security, and Public Policy Working Group at the International Security Program and as Associate Editor of the journal International Security . _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: Kelly M. Greenhill is a research fellow at the Belfer Center's International Security Program and a tenured associate professor at Tufts University. Her research expertise encompasses foreign policy, the use of military force, and 'new security challenges.' These challenges include civil wars, the strategic use of forced migration as a weapon, military intervention and counter-insurgency, and international crime as a threat to domestic governance. She holds advanced degrees from MIT and Harvard and a BA from UC Berkeley. Greenhill also chairs the Conflict, Security, and Public Policy Working Group and is an associate editor for the journal International Security. The article references her appearance on the 'Office Hours' podcast, situating her work within broader discussions on conflict resolution and international security.
result.main_content_zh: 凯莉·M·格林希尔是贝尔弗中心国际安全项目的研究员，也是塔夫茨大学的终身副教授。她的研究专注于外交政策、军事力量的使用以及所谓的“新安全挑战”。这些挑战包括内战、将强迫移民作为武器的策略性运用、军事干预与反叛乱，以及作为国内治理威胁的国际犯罪。她拥有麻省理工学院和哈佛大学的高级学位，以及加州大学伯克利分校的政治经济学和斯堪的纳维亚研究学士学位。格林希尔还担任国际安全项目中冲突、安全与公共政策工作小组的主席，并且是《国际安全》期刊的副主编。本文提及她参与“Office Hours”播客节目，将她的研究置于冲突解决和国际安全的更广泛讨论框架内。
